content:
- Completed task `task-02-yaml-config-secrets`: added strict YAML config loading in `internal/config`, wired `-config` into `app.ParseConfig`, and covered literal/env-ref/secret-file credentials plus validation/TLS path-only behavior with TDD tests.
- Verified all required gates passed: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all green; no `make test-long` run because this task did not require the long lane.
- Marked the task done with `<passes>true</passes>`, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed as `5e7bcf2`, pushed `master`, and should quit immediately.

- Read prior progress entry showing task-02-yaml-config-secrets completed and task switch already performed.
- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md to resolve blockers under choose-task rules.
- Determined the only unblocked failing task is .ralph/tasks/story-01-foundation/task-03-database-sql-connector.md; next is to verify no higher-priority bug/meta exception applies and write .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Refreshed .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md with .ralph/update_task_summary.sh and re-read .ralph/ralph-choose-task.md.
- Confirmed there are no active bug tasks and exactly one unblocked failing task: .ralph/tasks/story-01-foundation/task-03-database-sql-connector.md. Every later task remains blocked by incomplete predecessors.
- Next step is to write that path to .ralph/current_task.txt and quit immediately per choose-task instructions.

- Wrote .ralph/current_task.txt with .ralph/tasks/story-01-foundation/task-03-database-sql-connector.md.
- Selection rationale: bug-first found no unblocked bug tasks; task-03 is the only unblocked failing task because task-02 is done and every later task is still blocked by unfinished predecessors.
- Next step after this chooser turn is for the implementation agent to execute task-03, then rerun make check, make test, and make lint before switching tasks again.

- Read prior Ralph progress, current task pointer, and required skill files (`tdd`, `improve-code-boundaries`).
- Confirmed the selected task is `.ralph/tasks/story-01-foundation/task-03-database-sql-connector.md`; next I am reading the task file to determine whether this pass is planning (`TO BE VERIFIED`) or execution (`NOW EXECUTE`).
- Need to inspect the story task state and repository boundaries before deciding whether to create/update a plan or start TDD execution.

- Determined task-03 is still at initial state, so this turn should create a separate plan file and wire it into the task file rather than start implementation.
- Read the prior plan format plus current `internal/config`, `internal/app`, and tests to locate the database boundary: validated source config already exists, while `app.Run` currently owns only HTTP startup/shutdown.
- Next I am checking the HTTP server package and module dependencies so the database connector plan lands in the right package and includes one concrete boundary-cleanup target.

- Created the planning artifact for task-03 at `.ralph/tasks/story-01-foundation/task-03-database-sql-connector_plans/2026-04-30-database-sql-connector-plan.md` and linked it from the task file.
- The plan is marked `NOW EXECUTE` and anchors the work around a new `internal/database` boundary that owns pgx stdlib adaptation, TLS material loading, and readiness ping behavior while keeping `config.Source` as the single canonical database settings shape.
- Next turn should execute the plan with vertical TDD slices only, then run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` before any task switch or commit. Quit immediately now per Ralph planning instructions.

- Read prior Ralph progress and confirmed task-03 is already in NOW EXECUTE state.
- Loaded the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions for this turn.
- Next I am reading the task file and linked execution plan, then I will implement it with vertical TDD slices and keep appending progress during the work.

- Read the task and linked execution plan; task-03 requires executing the planned `internal/database` boundary now.
- Next I am inspecting the current `internal/app` and `internal/config` code/tests to place the first red test on the actual public interface and avoid inventing redundant config shapes.

- Inspected `internal/app` and `internal/config`; config already exposes a validated `config.Source` with TLS path fields, so the database package can consume it directly without a second config DTO.
- Next I am checking the remaining helpers, module deps, and any existing database/readiness references before writing the first failing database test.

- The current app bootstrap still owns only HTTP startup/shutdown, so I am checking nearby tasks and HTTP code before deciding whether this task should open/ping the database during startup or just provide the reusable database/readiness boundary.
- After that decision, I will start the first red TDD slice in `internal/database`.

- Added the first `internal/database` public test and confirmed the initial RED state with `go test ./internal/database` failing because the package has no implementation yet.
- Next I am adding the pgx stdlib dependency and inspecting the local adapter API so `database.Open` can build a `database/sql` handle from `config.Source` without any raw connection-string path.

- Implemented the first green slice: `internal/database.Open` now builds a private pgx config from `config.Source` and returns a `database/sql` handle through `stdlib.OpenDB`.
- Verified the first slice with `go test ./internal/database`.
- Next I am adding TLS red tests and inspecting the pgx adapter's TLS expectations so hostname verification stays correct while the database package owns file loading.

- Completed the database package TDD slices so far: minimal `Open`, TLS file-path loading with upfront failures, and `CheckReadiness` success/failure behavior with direct Go error text.
- Next I am reviewing the full diff and project make targets before running the required gate commands (`make check`, `make lint`, `make test`).

- Reviewed the diff and found the normal post-implementation cleanup items: gofmt still needs to run on the new database files, and `go mod tidy` should normalize the new pgx dependency metadata.
- After that cleanup I will run the required gates exactly as requested: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.

- All required task-03 gates are green: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed; no `make test-long` run because this task did not require the long lane.
- Marked the task and execution plan complete. Next I am running `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, then I will add all files, commit with evidence in the message, and push.
